metadata.comparison: V3 (problem-first phases) vs V2 (grill-me flat) vs V1 (procedural)

question_has_options.notes: V3: a/b/c options. V2: inline options. Both good.

product_depth_before_tech.notes: NEW. V3 says 'I want to understand the full picture before I write anything' and asks 'what's the real use case'. V2 asked same-depth product question. V3 explicitly frames as Phase A.

no_tech_in_first_question.notes: Neither asks about stack in first question

question_has_options.notes: V3: 4 options a-d + asks 'who actually uses it'. V2: similar but less structured.

product_depth_before_tech.notes: V3 asks 'What's the right framing? And who actually uses it'. More probing than V2.

short_known_list.notes: V3: 5 bullets but more concise — first bullet says 'essentially empty'. V2 had 5 verbose bullets. Marginal improvement.

question_has_options.notes: STILL no options for empty project. Says 'explain like you'd tell a friend'. V1 had A-E options. Persistent regression.

mentions_phase_a.notes: V3 says 'Question 1 of Phase A'. V2 didn't reference phases.

summary.v1_pass_rate: 76% (V1 had fewer assertions but lower rate)
improvements_v3: Explicitly references Phase A — sets expectation that product exploration comes first
Python question slightly deeper: 'who actually uses it — one person, a team, or end customers?'
Next.js: 'I want to understand the full picture before I write anything' — clear intent
Empty project mentions Phase A explicitly
persistent_issues: Empty project still no options in first question (V1 regression persists through V2 and V3)
Can only evaluate first question — full interview depth requires multi-turn testing

timing.notes: V3 marginally faster than V2, both much faster than V1
